candidate_source_schema.url_requirement: Use a stable public http(s) URL. Do not use private accounts, cookies, tokens, paywall-only material, screenshots, raw captures, or generated summaries.

candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Direct public project documentation for OpenClaw interface/channel claims, including Web Control UI, WebChat, mobile nodes, and gateway architecture.
candidate_sources.limitations: This page supports OpenClaw web/mobile access generally; it does not by itself prove a Hermes Agent-specific interface.
candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: The docs describe OpenClaw as a gateway across chat apps, WebChat, mobile nodes, and a Web Control UI, which can corroborate the web/phone interface part of the item.
candidate_sources.safe_summary: OpenClaw docs describe a self-hosted multi-channel gateway for AI agents with WebChat, mobile nodes, a Web Control UI, and phone-reachable messaging channels.
candidate_sources.title: OpenClaw documentation home

candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Direct public documentation for the browser-based OpenClaw Control UI.
candidate_sources.limitations: Supports the web-interface portion only; it does not prove phone/mobile or Hermes-specific claims.
candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: The item asks whether OpenClaw documentation supports a web interface; this page documents the browser Control UI and its local URLs/auth behavior.
candidate_sources.safe_summary: The Control UI is documented as a browser-based Gateway control UI for chat, activity, nodes, config, logs, updates, and related operations.
candidate_sources.title: Control UI

candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Direct public documentation for WebChat and macOS/iOS chat UI behavior.
candidate_sources.limitations: The page supports OpenClaw WebChat/mobile UI behavior but not necessarily Hermes Agent-specific use.
candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: The item asks about phone/mobile interface support; this page documents WebChat and notes macOS/iOS SwiftUI chat UI communicating with the Gateway WebSocket.
candidate_sources.safe_summary: OpenClaw WebChat docs describe a native macOS/iOS chat UI and Control UI chat tab using the Gateway WebSocket and shared sessions/routing.
candidate_sources.title: WebChat

candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Direct public documentation for iOS/Android/headless nodes and device capabilities.
candidate_sources.limitations: Nodes are peripherals rather than the primary user chat interface; this is context rather than direct proof of the original broad statement.
candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: The item asks about mobile/phone-related interfaces; node docs give public context for iOS/Android companion nodes and Gateway pairing.
candidate_sources.safe_summary: OpenClaw node docs describe companion devices including macOS, iOS, Android, and headless nodes that connect to the Gateway WebSocket and expose capabilities.
candidate_sources.title: Nodes

candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Public OpenClaw documentation mentioning Hermes in a migration context.
candidate_sources.limitations: Does not prove OpenClaw is a web/phone interface for Hermes Agent; it only documents migration from Hermes to OpenClaw.
candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: This candidate helps bound the Hermes-specific part of the item by showing an official OpenClaw/Hermes relationship, but only as migration support.
candidate_sources.safe_summary: OpenClaw migration docs describe a bundled Hermes migration provider that previews and imports selected Hermes state into OpenClaw.
candidate_sources.title: Migrating from Hermes to OpenClaw

candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Official CLI documentation for migration providers, including Hermes.
candidate_sources.limitations: Supports Hermes as a migration source/integration context, not necessarily as an execution environment or runtime dependency.
candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: The item asks whether OpenClaw docs/code explicitly name Hermes as a relevant environment, dependency, integration target, or adjacent operating context; this page explicitly documents the Hermes migration provider and commands.
candidate_sources.safe_summary: The OpenClaw migrate CLI docs list Hermes as a bundled migration provider and document dry-run/apply commands for importing Hermes state.
candidate_sources.title: openclaw migrate

candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Official migration guide dedicated to Hermes-to-OpenClaw migration.
candidate_sources.limitations: Does not show Hermes as a runtime dependency; it supports migration/adjacency only.
candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: This page directly names Hermes and explains what OpenClaw imports from Hermes, supporting an adjacent tooling/migration relationship.
candidate_sources.safe_summary: The guide describes previewed, reversible import from Hermes, including model config, MCP servers, workspace files, memory, skills, and selected credential handling.
candidate_sources.title: Migrating from Hermes to OpenClaw

candidate_sources.candidate_relevance: Official setup CLI documentation that references Hermes import during onboarding/setup.
candidate_sources.limitations: This is a setup/import option reference and does not establish a broad Hermes operating environment claim.
candidate_sources.reason_for_inclusion: The setup docs mention --import-from hermes and --import-source ~/.hermes, which corroborates a Hermes-adjacent onboarding path.
candidate_sources.safe_summary: The setup docs include examples for onboarding/import from Hermes and note that interactive onboarding can offer migration when Hermes state is detected.
candidate_sources.title: openclaw setup

curation_instructions: Fill candidate_sources either at the top level or inside each item, then set ready_for_import=true only after real bounded public candidates are added.
Do not invent URLs. Do not perform uncontrolled browsing. Prefer primary/official/academic/reputable public sources.
Do not include social media, SEO farms, unsourced posts, AI summaries, private/raw captures, screenshots, or unattributed material as standalone corroboration.
After filling, rerun Run 14A with --candidate-sources-json pointing to the expected candidate JSON path.

required_source_types: Code references to Hermes or Hermes Agent
